meaning: (bound form) false; erroneous; absurd; mu (Greek letter Μ, μ)
mnemonic: Speech with feathers flying away scatters false words into the air—absurd and erroneous, disappearing without truth.
A ritual vessel divides blood for ancient sacrifice, but when mishandled it sparks a bloody quarrel and dispute.
When the sun loves too dimly, daylight becomes obscure and clandestine affairs happen in the shadows.

meaning: to be versed in; to know well
mnemonic: Speech matching every sound perfectly—you're so versed in it, you know it well and can recite from memory.
A woman greedily hoarding an entire forest for herself shows avaricious desire—wanting everything, never satisfied.
